1386	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}
1387	  % (the complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$) (read from
1388	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1142--1162, with the $j_{p!}$ description
1389	  % L1095--1135).
1390	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider the chain complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$
1391	  % of presheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
1392	  % $$
1393	  % \ldots
1394	  % \to
1395	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1i_2} (j_{i_0i_1i_2})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1i_2}}
1396	  % \to
1397	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1} (j_{i_0i_1})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1}}
1398	  % \to
1399	  % \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}}
1400	  % \to 0 \to \ldots
1401	  % $$
1402	  % where the last nonzero term is placed in degree $0$
1403	  % and where the map
1404	  % $$
1405	  % (j_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}}}
1406	  % \longrightarrow
1407	  % (j_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}
1408	  % \mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}}}
1409	  % $$
1410	  % is given by $(-1)^j$ times the canonical map."
1411	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1412	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into} (the free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\)).}
1413	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1414	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
1415	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
1416	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
1417	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
1418	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
1419	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
1420	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
1421	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
1422	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
1423	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
1424	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
1425	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
1426	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
1427	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
1428	  \[
1429	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
1430	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1431	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
1432	  \]
1433	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
1434	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
1435	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
1436	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
1437	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
1438	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
1439	  \[
1440	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
1441	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1442	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1443	  \]
1444	  with \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) placed in degree \(p \geq 0\) (and \(0\) in negative
1445	  degrees), and whose differential \(K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1} \to K(\mathcal{U})_p\) is
1446	  the alternating sum \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\) of the maps
1447	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to
1448	  \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}})\)
1449	  obtained by applying \(\operatorname{free}\) to the representable index-dropping
1450	  maps \(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \to \mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1451	  \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\) induced by the inclusions of opens
1452	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \subset U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\)
1453	  that drop the index \(i_j\). Each summand is a \emph{projective} object of
1454	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\), because the universal property above identifies
1455	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1456	  i_p}), -)\) with the exact ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1457	  \((-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\); this is the structural feature exploited below.
1458	
1459	  We assume the index set \(I\) is \emph{finite} (so the required coproducts exist in
1460	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of the
1461	  comparison target). Here the direct sum \(\bigoplus\) is the categorical coproduct
1462	  \(\coprod\) (the indexed \(\operatorname{Sigma}\) colimit), which for a finite index
1463	  set coincides with the finite biproduct.
1464	
1465	  The parallel \texttt{...Fam} declarations pinned above are the cover-agnostic
1466	  raw-finite-family mirror of this construction — indexed by an arbitrary finite
1467	  family \((U_i)_{i \in I}\) of opens carrying \emph{no} covering hypothesis — and
1468	  feed the family form of the {\v C}ech acyclicity lemma
1469	  (\(\operatorname{injective\_cech\_acyclicFam}\)).
1470	\end{definition}

1504	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of presheaves.}
1505	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1506	  covering. For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), the
1507	  \emph{section {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1508	  \mathcal{F})\) is the cochain complex of \emph{abelian groups} whose term in
1509	  degree \(p\) is the product of sections over the \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
1510	  \[
1511	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1512	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1513	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1514	  \]
1515	  each factor being the value of the ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1516	  (the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)), with
1517	  differential the alternating sum of restriction maps
1518	  \[
1519	    d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1520	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
1521	        s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}
1522	        \big|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}.
1523	  \]
1524	  This object is \emph{distinct} from the relative {\v C}ech complex
1525	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
1526	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}: the latter is the pushforward complex with terms
1527	  \(\prod (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\) in
1528	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\), an object of relative sheaves over the base, whereas the
1529	  section {\v C}ech complex here is the simpler complex of \emph{global sections} of
1530	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over the intersections, an object of abelian groups (the underlying
1531	  additive structure of the sections). The two must not be conflated: the
1532	  presheaf-level acyclicity argument below runs entirely on this section complex.

1612	  \mathcal{F}(V)\), the natural identification, for each multi-index,

1616	    \bigr)

1624	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). Taking the product over multi-indices,

1953	  Fix an open \(V\) with \(I_1(V) = \{\, i : V \leq U_i \,\} \neq \varnothing\), and
1954	  let \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) be the single coefficient module
1955	  (\(\operatorname{coverSectionModule}\)). The \emph{engine chain complex}
1956	  \(C_\bullet = \operatorname{cechEngineComplex}\, \mathcal{U}\, V\) is the chain
1957	  complex of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-modules with
1958	  \[
1959	    C_p \;=\; \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1960	    \qquad (\operatorname{cechEngineX}),
1961	  \]
1962	  the coproduct taken in \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(\mathcal{O}_X(V))\), and with
1963	  differential \(d = \operatorname{cechEngineD}\) given on the injection
1964	  \(\iota_\sigma\) of the index \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)\) by the
1965	  alternating sum of index-drop reindexings,
1966	  \[
1967	    d \circ \iota_\sigma
1968	      \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{p} (-1)^i\, \iota_{\sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i}
1969	    \qquad (\operatorname{cechEngineD\_\iota}),
1970	  \]
1971	  i.e.\ the chain transpose of the index-\emph{raise} differential
1972	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\) of
1973	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}. Then:
1974	  \begin{itemize}
1975	    \item \(d \circ d = 0\) (\(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_comp}\)), so the family
1976	      assembles into the chain complex \(C_\bullet = \operatorname{ChainComplex.of}\)
1977	      (\(\operatorname{cechEngineComplex}\)).
1978	    \item The \emph{prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)} map
1979	      \(s \mapsto \iota_{\operatorname{Fin.cons}\, i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, \sigma}\)
1980	      (\(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend}\), with its injection computation
1981	      \(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend\_\iota}\)) is a contracting homotopy in
1982	      positive degree: \(s \circ d + d \circ s = \mathrm{id}\)
1983	      (\(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend\_spec}\)).
1984	    \item Hence \(C_\bullet\) is exact in positive degree,
1985	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(\operatorname{cechEngineD}(n+1),
1986	      \operatorname{cechEngineD} n)\) (\(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_exact}\)).
1987	  \end{itemize}
1988	  Four object-half bricks realise the degreewise identification
1989	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong C_p\) consumed by
1990	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}: evaluation commutes with the coproduct;
1991	  the non-surviving summands (those \(\sigma\) with \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\)) drop to the
1992	  zero object (\(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\),
1993	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalEngine\_X}\)); and the surviving index type is reindexed
1994	  by the equivalence
1995	  \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv} : (\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)) \simeq
1996	  \{\, \sigma \;//\; V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma \,\}\),
1997	  where the membership criterion
1998	  \(V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma
1999	  \iff \forall k,\ V \leq U_{\sigma(k)}\) is
2000	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\).
2001	
2002	  The parallel \texttt{...Fam} engine declarations pinned above are the cover-agnostic
2003	  raw-finite-family mirror of this construction (an arbitrary finite family of opens,
2004	  no covering hypothesis), feeding the family form of the {\v C}ech acyclicity lemma.
2005	\end{lemma}
2006	
2007	\begin{proof}
2008	  \uses{lem:free_cech_engine}
2009	  The complex \(C_\bullet\) is the chain/coproduct dual of the constant-coefficient
2010	  core of Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}. The differential
2011	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) is defined on coproduct injections by the
2012	  alternating-sign index-drop \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove}
2013	  i\); both \(d \circ d = 0\) and the contracting identity
2014	  \(s \circ d + d \circ s = \mathrm{id}\) are proved by extending along the
2015	  injections with \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\) and reducing each
2016	  injection-component to the corresponding combinatorial cancellation of
2017	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential\_comp}\) respectively
2018	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\) (the prepend
2019	  \(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend}\) is the coproduct dual of
2020	  \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\)). Positive-degree exactness
2021	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_exact}\) reads off the contracting identity. The four
2022	  object-half bricks are immediate: evaluation preserves \(\coprod\) and the surviving
2023	  summands are picked out by \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) under the criterion
2024	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\), the rest being zero objects
2025	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\)).
2026	\end{proof}
2027	
2028	\begin{lemma}
2029	
2030	[Degreewise identification of the evaluated free complex with the engine complex]
2031	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}
2032	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngineIso,
2033	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeAug_eval_eq,
2034	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngineIso_hom_f,
2035	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngine_X_inv_hom_ι,
2036	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngine_comm,
2037	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngine_map_ι,
2038	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_X_ι_inv,
2039	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFree_d_ι,
2040	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaAug_app_comp,
2041	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_hom_eq_aug,
2042	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_natural,
2043	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngineIsoFam,
2044	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeAug_eval_eqFam,
2045	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngineIso_hom_fFam,
2046	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngine_X_inv_hom_ιFam,
2047	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngine_commFam,
2048	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngine_map_ιFam,
2049	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_X_ι_invFam,
2050	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFree_d_ιFam}
2051	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine, lem:cech_engine_complex}
2052	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2053	  % (proof, the description of $K^{ext}_p(W)$ and its differential) (read from
2054	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1236--1251).
2055	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
2056	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$
2057	  % and
2058	  % $$
2059	  % K^{ext}_p(W) =
2060	  % \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W).
2061	  % $$
2062	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
2063	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$.
2064	  % Moreover, the differential of the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$
2065	  % is given by
2066	  % $$
2067	  % d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} =
2068	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
2069	  % (-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
2070	  % $$"
2071	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2072	  (the evaluated complex and its differential).}
2073	  Fix an open \(V\) with \(I_1(V) = \{\, i : V \leq U_i \,\} \neq \varnothing\). There
2074	  is an isomorphism of chain complexes of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-modules
2075	  \[
2076	    \bigl((\operatorname{eval} V).\operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\bigr)
2077	      \bigl(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex} \mathcal{U}\bigr)
2078	    \;\cong\;
2079	    C_\bullet,
2080	    \qquad
2081	    C_p = \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V),
2082	  \]
2083	  where the coproduct is taken in \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(\mathcal{O}_X(V))\) and the
2084	  differential of \(C_\bullet\) is the constant-coefficient {\v C}ech differential
2085	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\) on the index set \(J = I_1(V)\)
2086	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}). The isomorphism is \emph{degreewise} and
2087	  \emph{intertwines the evaluated alternating-face differential with}
2088	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\). It is the chain/coproduct dual of the section-side
2089	  cochain identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}; isolating it
2090	  here gives the empty and nonempty cases (Lemmas~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}
2091	  and~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}) a common, ready degreewise model.
2092	
2093	  The parallel \texttt{...Fam} declarations pinned above are the cover-agnostic
2094	  raw-finite-family mirror of this degreewise identification (an arbitrary finite
2095	  family of opens, no covering hypothesis), feeding the family form of the {\v C}ech
2096	  acyclicity lemma.
2097	\end{lemma}
2098	
2099	\begin{proof}
2100	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine}
2101	  \emph{Degreewise.} By \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\) of
2102	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} evaluation at \(V\) commutes with the
2103	  coproduct,
2104	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong \coprod_{\sigma :
2105	  \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \mathcal{U}.I_0} (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}
2106	  (\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))\). Split the index set into the \(\sigma\) landing in
2107	  \(I_1(V)\) and the rest. Each summand with \(\sigma\) valued in \(I_1(V)\) (so
2108	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\)) is \(\cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) by
2109	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\); each remaining summand is the zero
2110	  object by \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\). Hence the coproduct
2111	  collapses to \(\coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V) =
2112	  C_p\).
2113	
2114	  \emph{Differential match.} The evaluated differential is \((\operatorname{eval} V)\)
2115	  applied to the alternating face sum
2116	  \(\operatorname{AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD}(\operatorname{cechFreeSimplicial}
2117	  \mathcal{U})\). The face \(\delta_i\) reindexes the \(\sigma\)-summand by
2118	  \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\) (the face action of
2119	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeSimplicial}\)); restricted to the
2120	  \(I_1\)-summands this is exactly the index-drop that the engine encodes, and the sign
2121	  \((-1)^i\) is supplied by \(\operatorname{objD}\). The match is verified on the
2122	  coproduct injections by \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\), reusing the
2123	  naturality of \(\operatorname{Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso}\); summing with signs
2124	  identifies the evaluated differential with
2125	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\). This is the hard content of the
2126	  identification and the chain/coproduct dual of the cochain coface match in
2127	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}.
2128	
2129	  \emph{The three layers commuted past.} The degreewise iso is the \(3\)-fold
2130	  composite
2131	  \[
2132	    (\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p)
2133	      \xrightarrow{\;\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\;}
2134	      \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \mathcal{U}.I_0}
2135	        (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))
2136	      \xrightarrow{\;\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\;}
2137	      \coprod_{V \leq U_\sigma} \mathcal{O}_X(V)
2138	      \xrightarrow{\;(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv}\ \ldots)^{-1}\;}
2139	      C_p,
2140	  \]
2141	  whose layers are: (1) \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\) = the comparison
2142	  \(\operatorname{Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso}\) expressing that evaluation at
2143	  \(V\) commutes with the coproduct \(\coprod\); (2) \(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\),
2144	  which kills the summands with \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\) (these evaluate to zero objects
2145	  by \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\)); (3)
2146	  \((\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv}\ \operatorname{survivingEquiv}\
2147	  (\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\, \ldots))^{-1}\), which reindexes the
2148	  surviving summands by \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) of
2149	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex} and identifies each evaluated summand with the
2150	  single coefficient \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) via \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\).
2151	
2152	  \emph{Naturality past each layer.} The reindex \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) is
2153	  natural in the face map \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\):
2154	  a surviving index \(\sigma\) (i.e.\ \(V \leq U_\sigma\), equivalently
2155	  \(V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma\) by
2156	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\)) is carried to a surviving
2157	  \(\sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\) (still \(V \leq U_{\sigma \circ
2158	  \operatorname{succAbove}\, i}\), since dropping a coordinate only weakens the meet),
2159	  so the drop-zeros layer (2) and the whisker layer (3) commute with every face. On a
2160	  surviving summand the evaluated transition \(\operatorname{freeYoneda}.\mathrm{map}
2161	  (\operatorname{homOfLE}\, \ldots)\) collapses to the identity under
2162	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\); hence each face \(\delta_i\) matches the
2163	  engine reindexing \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\)
2164	  summand-for-summand, and summing with the \(\operatorname{objD}\) signs \((-1)^i\)
2165	  identifies the evaluated alternating-face differential with
2166	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) (\(= \) the chain transpose of
2167	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\)).
2168	
2169	  \emph{The genuine difficulty: variance.} The free \emph{chain} differential
2170	  \emph{lowers} \(\mathrm{Fin}\)-arity (\(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ
2171	  \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\), faces), whereas
2172	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\) \emph{raises} it (it inserts an
2173	  index, summing over \(i \in I_1\)). The two are reconciled because on the finite
2174	  biproduct \(\coprod = \prod\) the insertion/deletion adjunction makes the index-drop
2175	  chain differential the transpose of the index-raise cochain differential; this
2176	  transpose is precisely what \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) of
2177	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex} packages, and verifying the commutation amounts to
2178	  the injectionwise check \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\) outlined above.
2179	\end{proof}
2180	
2181	\begin{lemma}
2182	
2183	[Evaluated free complex over an open meeting no cover member]
2184	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}
2185	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty,
2186	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty,
2187	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty,
2188	    AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_homology_of_isZero_X,
2189	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmptyFam,
2190	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmptyFam,
2191	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmptyFam}
2192	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine}
2193	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2194	  % (proof, the empty case) (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex,
2195	  % L1228--1230).
2196	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $I_1 = \emptyset$, then the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W) = 0$
2197	  % so there is nothing to prove."
2198	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2199	  (empty case).}

6013	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherence implies the counit is an isomorphism (01I8, Route-A fallback)]
6014	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_quasicoherent}
6015	  \uses{lem:qcoh_global_generation, lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh,
6016	    lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_genSections}
6017	  % NOTE: Route-A fallback, superseded by Route B. The live formalization of the 01I8 counit-iso
6018	  % instance AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent is the section-localization
6019	  % assembly Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}; this block carries the dormant
6020	  % global-generation proof of the same instance (no \lean{} pin, to avoid duplicating the target).
6021	  % See analogies/o1i8-route.md.
6022	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Tag 01I8, Lemma
6023	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex,
6024	  % L1278--1387).
6025	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
6026	  % be an affine scheme. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a
6027	  % quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Then
6028	  % $\mathcal{F}$ is isomorphic to the sheaf associated to
6029	  % the $R$-module $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})$."
6030	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01I8,
6031	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}.}
6032	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
6033	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
6034	  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma} : \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\)
6035	  is an isomorphism. This is the affine global-generation instance
6036	  (\([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
6037	  \operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\), Stacks Tag 01I8) that
6038	  discharges the conditional hypothesis of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}.
6039	  \emph{This is the Route-A (global-generation) proof of the 01I8 counit isomorphism; it is a
6040	  dormant fallback superseded by the live section-localization assembly
6041	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma} (Route B; see \texttt{analogies/o1i8-route.md}).}
6042	\end{lemma}
6043	\begin{proof}
6044	  \uses{lem:qcoh_global_generation, lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh,
6045	    lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_genSections}
6046	  Quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) gives, by Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation}, a
6047	  global generating family \(\sigma\). Its kernel \(\operatorname{Ker} \sigma.\pi\) is
6048	  again quasi-coherent and hence globally generated
6049	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh}), yielding a generating family \(\tau\). Feeding the
6050	  pair \((\sigma, \tau)\) to Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_genSections} makes
6051	  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) an isomorphism.
6052	\end{proof}
6053	
6054	\begin{lemma}
6055	[Standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing for quasi-coherent coefficients]
6056	  \label{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh}
6057	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh,
6058	    AlgebraicGeometry.affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing}
6059	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections, lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover,
6060	    lem:cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso, def:affine_cover_system, def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}
6061	  % NOTE: this target is currently formalized in the REDUCED form
6062	  % `AlgebraicGeometry.affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` (axiom-clean), which transports
6063	  % the seed to the tilde case via `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (and the transport helper
6064	  % `cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso`) and bottoms out at the explicit residual
6065	  % `lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover` (passed as the hypothesis `htilde`). The unconditional
6066	  % `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` is obtained once that residual is a closed Lean declaration.
6067	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG, Lemma
6068	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (proof, condition (3)) (read
6069	  % from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L172--173).
6070	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
6071	  % Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
6072	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG
6073	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, proof).}
6074	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
6075	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for the
6076	  affine cover system (Definition~\ref{def:affine_cover_system},
6077	  Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}): for every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\) and every
6078	  \(p > 0\) one has \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This supplies condition (3) of the
6079	  basis-comparison Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}, via the tilde-case residual
6080	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover}.
6081	\end{lemma}
6082	
6083	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write $U = \Spec(A)$ for some ring $A$.
6084	% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
6085	% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
6086	% Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
6087	% Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
6088	% $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
6089	% is identified with the complex
6090	% $\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to \ldots$"
6091	\begin{proof}
6092	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections,
6093	    lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover, lem:cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso}
6094	  \emph{Step 1 (reduction to the tilde case).} By Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} the
6095	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is isomorphic to \(\widetilde{M}\) for
6096	  \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\), with sections over each face \(D(g_\sigma)\) the away localisation
6097	  \(M_{g_\sigma}\). Since {\v C}ech cohomology transports along an isomorphism of coefficient
6098	  presheaves (Lemma~\ref{lem:cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso}), it suffices to prove the vanishing for
6099	  the tilde sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\): for every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\) and every \(p > 0\),
6100	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \widetilde{M}) = 0\).
6101	
6102	  \emph{Step 2 (the tilde residual).} A standard cover of the affine \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) in
6103	  the affine cover system is a cover of a distinguished open \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) by smaller
6104	  distinguished opens; for a proper \(D(f)\) the family \(\{g_i\}\) does \emph{not} span the unit
6105	  ideal of \(R\), so the whole-space standard-cover vanishing of
6106	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (stated under \(\operatorname{span}\{g_i\} = R\)) does not
6107	  apply directly. The required positive-degree vanishing \(\check{H}^p(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}) = 0\)
6108	  over such a proper cover is exactly Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover}, proved by
6109	  change of base to \(R_f = \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\) (where the cover \emph{does} span the
6110	  unit ideal). Combining Steps 1 and 2 gives \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all
6111	  \(p > 0\) and every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\); hence \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing higher
6112	  {\v C}ech cohomology for the affine cover system.
6113	\end{proof}

6115	\begin{lemma}[{\v C}ech cohomology transports along an isomorphism of coefficients]
6116	  \label{lem:cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso}
6117	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso}
6118	  \uses{def:cech_cohomology_accessor, def:section_cech_functoriality}
6119	  \textit{Project-local transport lemma.}
6120	  Let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family and let \(e : \mathcal{F} \cong
6121	  \mathcal{G}\) be an isomorphism of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. If
6122	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for some \(p\), then
6123	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{G}) = 0\).
6124	\end{lemma}
6125	\begin{proof}
6126	  \uses{def:section_cech_functoriality}
6127	  The section {\v C}ech complex is functorial in the coefficient presheaf
6128	  (Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_functoriality}), so \(e\) induces an isomorphism of section
6129	  {\v C}ech complexes \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong
6130	  \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{G})\), and applying the degree-\(p\) homology
6131	  functor yields an isomorphism \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong
6132	  \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{G})\). A group isomorphic to a zero object is itself zero.
6133	\end{proof}

6135	\begin{lemma}[A finite family of distinguished opens covers \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)
6136	  iff its elements span the unit ideal]
6137	  \label{lem:iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff_span}
6138	  \lean{PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff}
6139	  \mathlibok
6140	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
6141	  For a family \(s : \iota \to R\), one has \(\bigsqcup_i D(s_i) = \operatorname{Spec} R\) if and only
6142	  if \(\operatorname{span}(\{s_i : i \in \iota\}) = R\).
6143	\end{lemma}

6145	\begin{lemma}[A standard cover of \(D(f)\) spans the unit ideal of \(R_f\)]
6146	  \label{lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway}
6147	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_cover_span_localizationAway}
6148	  \uses{lem:iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff_span}
6149	  \textit{Project-local infrastructure.}
6150	  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(f \in R\), and \(g : \iota \to R\) a finite family with \(D(f) =
6151	  \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). Then the images
6152	  \(\operatorname{algebraMap} R\, R_f\, (g_i)\) span the unit ideal of \(R_f =
6153	  \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\):
6154	  \[
6155	    \operatorname{span}\bigl(\{\, g_i/1 : i \in \iota \,\}\bigr) = R_f.
6156	  \]
6157	\end{lemma}
6158	\begin{proof}
6159	  Pull the equality \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) back along the canonical open immersion
6160	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f \cong D(f) \hookrightarrow \operatorname{Spec} R\), whose underlying
6161	  continuous map is the comap of \(\operatorname{algebraMap} R\, R_f\). Comap commutes with
6162	  distinguished opens and with suprema, so the pullback of \(\bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) is
6163	  \(\bigsqcup_i D(g_i/1)\), while the pullback of \(D(f)\) is all of \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f\)
6164	  because \(f\) is a unit in \(R_f\). Thus \(\bigsqcup_i D(g_i/1) = \operatorname{Spec} R_f\), which
6165	  by the span criterion (a family of distinguished opens covers the whole spectrum iff the elements
6166	  generate the unit ideal) is equivalent to \(\operatorname{span}\{g_i/1\} = R_f\).
6167	\end{proof}

6174	  \(M_a = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} a)\, M\) for the away

6176	  exhibits \(M_{ab}\) as the localisation of \(M_a\) at (the image of) \(b\):
6177	  \[
6178	    M_a\bigl[1/b\bigr] \;=\; M_{ab},
6179	  \]

6181	  \(ab\). In particular, it yields the degreewise identification

6183	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover}: when \(f\) is invertible in \(M_{g_\sigma}\)
6184	  (because \(g_\sigma \in \sqrt{(f)}\)), localising \(M\) away from \(g_\sigma\) and localising
6185	  \(M_f\) away from \(g_\sigma\) present the same module.

6210	  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(M\) an \(R\)-module, \(f \in R\), and \(g : \{1, \ldots, n\} \to R\) a finite
6211	  family with \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). Then for every \(p > 0\),

6218	% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
6219	% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
6220	% Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
6221	% Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
6222	% $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
6223	% is identified with the complex
6224	% $\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to \ldots$"

6226	  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact, lem:section_cech_homology_exact,
6227	    lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway, lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule}
6228	  Set \(R_f = \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\) and \(M_f =
6229	  \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} f)\, M\). Following the Stacks proof's
6230	  ``Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\)'' step, we run the whole computation over the ring \(R_f\),
6231	  where the cover \emph{does} span the unit ideal, and transport the resulting exactness back to \(R\)
6232	  algebraically.
6233	
6234	  \emph{The \(R_f\)-side complex is exact.} The un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex
6235	  \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} is polymorphic in the base ring:
6236	  for any commutative ring, any module over it, and any spanning family of the unit ideal, its
6237	  positive-degree exactness is Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}. Instantiate that lemma over
6238	  \(R_f\) with the module \(M_f\) and the family \(g/1 : \iota \to R_f\),
6239	  \((g/1)_i = \operatorname{algebraMap} R\, R_f\, (g_i)\). Over \(R_f\) this family spans the unit
6240	  ideal (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway}, using \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\)), so
6241	  the spanning hypothesis of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} is met and the \(R_f\)-side
6242	  complex \(D^\bullet_{R_f}\) --- whose degree-\(p\) term is \(\prod_\sigma (M_f)_{(g/1)_\sigma}\) ---
6243	  is exact in positive degrees.
6244	
6245	  \emph{Degreewise transfer to the \(R\)-side.} For a tuple \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) put
6246	  \(g_\sigma = \prod_k g_{\sigma k}\). Since each \(D(g_i) \subseteq D(f)\) we have \(D(g_\sigma)
6247	  \subseteq D(f)\), hence \(g_\sigma \in \sqrt{(f)}\) and \(f\) is invertible in the away localisation
6248	  \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The transitivity comparison of
6249	  Lemma~\ref{lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule} therefore exhibits a canonical isomorphism of
6250	  abelian groups
6251	  \[
6252	    M_{g_\sigma} \;\cong\; (M_f)_{g_\sigma},
6253	  \]
6254	  both sides presenting \(M\) localised away from \(g_\sigma f\). These per-tuple isomorphisms are the
6255	  vertical maps of a ladder between the \(R\)-side section {\v C}ech module complex of \(\widetilde{M}\)
6256	  over \(\{D(g_i)\}\) and the \(R_f\)-side complex \(D^\bullet_{R_f}\). They commute with the
6257	  alternating-sum localisation differentials, because each {\v C}ech coface is itself an
6258	  away-localisation comparison map and the comparisons of
6259	  Lemma~\ref{lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule} are natural in the localisation base (the same
6260	  naturality square intertwining the complex differential with the section cofaces in
6261	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}). Transporting positive-degree exactness across this
6262	  additive ladder --- an exactness transfer along an isomorphism of complexes --- yields positive-degree
6263	  exactness for the \(R\)-side section {\v C}ech differentials.
6264	
6265	  \emph{From exactness to vanishing homology.} Wrapping positive-degree function exactness as the
6266	  vanishing of the degree-\(p\) homology --- exactly as Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
6267	  does for the whole-space case --- gives \(\check{H}^p(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}) = 0\) for all
6268	  \(p > 0\).
6269	\end{proof}

6272	(Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) is proved by a dimension shift on the
6273	coefficient module, decomposed below into four sub-lemmas: the short exact sequence of
6274	{\v C}ech complexes coming from a basis (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}), the
6275	resulting stability of vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology under the injective
6276	quotient (Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}), the sheaf-cohomology base case
6277	\(H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}), and the
6278	dimension-shift induction \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\)
6279	(Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}). The top lemma's proof then just
6280	chains these.

6282	\subsection{Section {\v C}ech infrastructure and the cover-system encoding}
6283	
6284	The sub-lemmas below are formalized in a \emph{cover-local}, presheaf-level, hypothesis-driven
6285	form: each lemma fixes a single index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) and works with the
6286	section {\v C}ech complexes of that one covering, taking the geometric inputs (section-level
6287	exactness on faces, positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing) as hypotheses. The cover-global
6288	\((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) data of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis} re-enters only
6289	in the inductive step (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}), where it is packaged by
6290	the cover-system encoding (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system},
6291	Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) and discharges those hypotheses via the per-face
6292	short-exact-sequence derivation (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}). The supporting
6293	definitions and the degreewise product engine are collected first.
6294	
6295	\begin{definition}
6296	
6297	\leanok
6298	[{\v C}ech cohomology accessor]
6299	  \label{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
6300	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechCohomology}
6301	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
6302	  \textit{Project-local accessor.}
6303	  For an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)), a
6304	  presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(p \geq 0\), the \emph{{\v C}ech
6305	  cohomology} \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the degree-\(p\) homology of the section
6306	  {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex},
6307	  \[
6308	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\;
6309	      H^p\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr),
6310	  \]
6311	  taken as an object of \(\mathrm{Ab}\). This is the named wrapper that the 01EO chain refers to
6312	  uniformly.
6313	\end{definition}

6315	\begin{definition}
6316	[Functoriality of the section {\v C}ech complex]
6317	  \label{def:section_cech_functoriality}
6318	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCosimplicialMap,
6319	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor,
6320	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexFunctor,
6321	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexMap}
6322	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}

6324	  A morphism \(\varphi : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G}\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
6325	  acts on the section {\v C}ech data of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} coordinatewise:
6326	  on the basic-open section group of each intersection it is the map
6327	  \(\varphi(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) : \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) \to \mathcal{G}(U_{i_0 \ldots
6328	  i_p})\). These assemble into a cosimplicial morphism (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial
6329	  map}), functorial in \(\varphi\) (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial functor}). Composing
6330	  with the alternating-coface-map complex functor packages the section {\v C}ech complex as a
6331	  functor in the coefficient presheaf (the \emph{section {\v C}ech complex functor}) and yields,
6332	  for each \(\varphi\), the induced chain map of section {\v C}ech complexes
6333	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \varphi)\). These are the maps used to build the short
6334	  complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex}.

6385	  \label{def:basis_cov_system}

6388	  \textit{Project-bespoke encoding of the hypotheses of Stacks 01EO.}
6389	  A \emph{cover system on a basis} for a scheme \(X\) is a record with five fields. A
6390	  \emph{covering datum} (the helper \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\)) is a pair \((\iota, U)\) of an index type
6391	  \(\iota\) together with an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\); it is the raw shape
6392	  \(\Sigma\,\iota,\ \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) over which the section {\v C}ech complex is built.
6393	  The cover system bundles:
6394	  \begin{enumerate}
6395	  \item a basis \(\mathcal{B} \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) for the topology;
6396	  \item a set \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of admissible coverings, each a \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\);
6397	  \item \emph{faces-in-basis} (\(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\)): for every covering in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) every
6398	    finite intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) of its opens lies in \(\mathcal{B}\) (condition (1) of
6399	    Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis});
6400	  \item \emph{section surjectivity} (\(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\)): for every \(V \in \mathcal{B}\)
6401	    and every short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to S_3 \to 0\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
6402	    whose left term \(S_1\) has vanishing positive {\v C}ech cohomology over the coverings of \(V\) in
6403	    \(\mathrm{Cov}\), the section map \(S_2(V) \to S_3(V)\) is surjective;
6404	  \item \emph{injective acyclicity} (\(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\)): for every injective
6405	    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) and every covering \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) the
6406	    positive-degree {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes, \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for
6407	    all \(p > 0\).
6408	  \end{enumerate}
6409	  Unlike a bare combinatorial record, the structure carries two sheaf-theoretic ({\v C}ech-cohomology)

6945	  the cover-system encoding. Conditions (1)--(2) of the hypotheses are exactly the faces-in-basis and
6946	  cofinality data of a cover system on a basis, so \(\mathcal{B}\) and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) assemble into
6947	  an instance \(s\) of Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system} with \(s.\mathcal{B} = \mathcal{B}\) and
6948	  \(s.\mathrm{Cov} = \mathrm{Cov}\); condition (3) is exactly the assertion that \(\mathcal{F}\) has
6949	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\) (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}).
6950	
6951	  The conclusion is then the dimension-shift induction
6952	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} applied to this instance: for every basic open
6953	  \(U \in s.\mathcal{B} = \mathcal{B}\) and every \(p > 0\) one has \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). That
6954	  induction internally embeds \(\mathcal{F}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{I}\), forms the quotient
6955	  \(\mathcal{Q} = \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), keeps \(\mathcal{Q}\) inside the inductive class via the
6956	  {\v C}ech short exact sequence (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}) and its quotient-stability
6957	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}), and bottoms out at the base case
6958	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}).
6959	
6960	  The argument never uses affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing
6961	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is condition (3),
6962	  which on the affine instantiation is supplied by the standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
6963	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. Thus the dependency
6964	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the graph is
6965	  acyclic.
6966	\end{proof}

6968	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
6969	
6970	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
6971	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
6972	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
6973	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
6974	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
6975	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
6976	
6977	\begin{lemma}
6978	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
6979	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
6980	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
6981	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
6982	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
6983	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
6984	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
6985	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
6986	  % \begin{equation}
6987	  % \label{equation-extended}
6988	  % 0 \to
6989	  % M \to
6990	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
6991	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
6992	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
6993	  % \ldots
6994	  % \end{equation}
6995	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
6996	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
6997	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
6998	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
6999	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
7000	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7001	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7002	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
7003	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
7004	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
7005	  \[
7006	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
7007	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
7008	    \qquad
7009	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
7010	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
7011	  \]
7012	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
7013	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
7014	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
7015	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
7016	\end{lemma}
7017	
7018	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
7019	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
7020	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
7021	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
7022	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
7023	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
7024	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. Note that
7025	% $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$. ...
7026	% Let us define a homotopy ... by the rule
7027	% $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$"
7028	% (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L80--110).
7029	\begin{proof}
7030	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
7031	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7032	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7033	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is a \emph{local}
7034	  question: the complex is exact if and only if it is exact on every stalk
7035	  (equivalently, after localizing the sections at every prime). It therefore
7036	  suffices to check exactness of the augmented {\v C}ech complex stalkwise.
7037	
7038	  Fix an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) of \(X\) and a prime
7039	  \(\mathfrak{p} \in \operatorname{Spec}(A)\). By the affine tilde isomorphism
7040	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} and \ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}) we have
7041	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U \cong \widetilde{M}\) with \(M = \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\), and the
7042	  sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over the faces are the away-localizations
7043	  \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the augmented {\v C}ech
7044	  complex over \(U\) is thus the localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the extended
7045	  complex of localizations
7046	  \[
7047	    0 \;\to\; M \;\to\; \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
7048	      \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\; \cdots.
7049	  \]
7050	  Since the \(\{f_i\}\) cover \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), for the prime
7051	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) there is an index \(i\) with \(f_i \notin \mathfrak{p}\); that is,
7052	  \(f_i\) becomes a \emph{unit} in \(A_{\mathfrak{p}}\). Fixing one such index
7053	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), the standard ``one index is a unit'' contracting homotopy
7054	  \[
7055	    h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \;=\; s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, i_0 \ldots i_p}
7056	  \]
7057	  witnesses that the localized extended complex is homotopic to zero, hence exact in
7058	  every positive degree and at the augmentation. This is precisely the standard-cover
7059	  {\v C}ech vanishing already established at the level of sections in
7060	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
7061	
7062	  As this holds for every prime \(\mathfrak{p}\) — hence on every stalk — the
7063	  augmented {\v C}ech complex is exact on \(X\); that is, the {\v C}ech nerve of
7064	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
7065	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
7066	\end{proof}

7068	\begin{lemma}
7069	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
7070	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7071	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
7072	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
7073	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
7074	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
7075	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
7076	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
7077	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
7078	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
7079	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
7080	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
7081	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
7082	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
7083	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
7084	  \[
7085	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
7086	      \;\cong\;
7087	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
7088	  \]
7089	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
7090	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
7091	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
7092	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
7093	\end{lemma}
7094	
7095	\begin{proof}
7096	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
7097	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
7098	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
7099	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
7100	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
7101	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
7102	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
7103	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
7104	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
7105	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
7106	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
7107	  additive homology-preserving functor.
7108	\end{proof}

7110	\begin{lemma}
7111	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
7112	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
7113	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
7114	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}
7115	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7116	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
7117	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
7118	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
7119	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
7120	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
7121	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
7122	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
7123	  % associated to the presheaves
7124	  % $$
7125	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
7126	  % $$
7127	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
7128	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
7129	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
7130	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
7131	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
7132	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
7133	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
7134	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
7135	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
7136	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
7137	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
7138	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
7139	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
7140	  \[
7141	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
7142	      \;\cong\;
7143	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
7144	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
7145	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
7146	  \]
7147	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
7148	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
7149	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
7150	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
7151	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
7152	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
7153	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
7154	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
7155	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
7156	
7157	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
7158	  of the preimage,
7159	  \[
7160	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
7161	      \;=\;
7162	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
7163	  \]
7164	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
7165	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
7166	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
7167	\end{lemma}
7168	
7169	\begin{proof}
7170	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7171	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
7172	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
7173	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
7174	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
7175	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
7176	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
7177	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
7178	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
7179	  \[
7180	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
7181	      \;\cong\;
7182	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
7183	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
7184	  \]
7185	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
7186	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
7187	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
7188	\end{proof}

7190	\begin{lemma}
7191	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
7192	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
7193	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
7194	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
7195	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
7196	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
7197	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
7198	  % L180--199).
7199	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
7200	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
7201	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
7202	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7203	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
7204	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
7205	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
7206	  \to S\). Then:
7207	  \begin{enumerate}
7208	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
7209	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
7210	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
7211	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
7212	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
7213	      \(k \geq 0\),
7214	      \[
7215	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
7216	      \]
7217	  \end{enumerate}
7218	\end{lemma}
7219	
7220	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
7221	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
7222	% the sheaf
7223	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
7224	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
7225	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
7226	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
7227	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
7228	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
7229	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
7230	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
7231	\begin{proof}
7232	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
7233	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
7234	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
7235	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
7236	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
7237	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
7238	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
7239	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
7240	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
7241	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
7242	
7243	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
7244	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
7245	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
7246	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
7247	
7248	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
7249	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
7250	  sheafification of
7251	  \[
7252	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
7253	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
7254	  \]
7255	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
7256	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
7257	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
7258	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
7259	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
7260	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
7261	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
7262	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
7263	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
7264	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
7265	
7266	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
7267	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
7268	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
7269	  for every \(k\),
7270	  \[
7271	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
7272	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
7273	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
7274	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
7275	  \]
7276	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
7277	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
7278	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
7279	\end{proof}

7306	
7307	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
7308	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
7309	% the sheaf
7310	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
7311	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
7312	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is

7316	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
7317	% opens we win."

7321	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
7322	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
7323	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived

7325	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
7326	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
7327	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :

content:
# Strategy

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak existence
form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), with `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 02KG `affine_serre_vanishing` (cover-system + affine instantiation) | ACTIVE (ungated iter-049) | ~1 | ~40–120 | Tops `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` (02KG seed) + `affine_serre_vanishing` consume the now-unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (01I8 closed iter-048). Both DISPATCHED iter-049 (same lane, seed→top). | Cover-system chain COMPLETE; both blocks blueprint-gate-cleared (reviewer iter049). Seed = transport P3 tilde-vanishing along `F≅~M`; top = instantiate 01EO basis criterion. `[EnoughInjectives]` carried as explicit hyp (connector deferred to P5b). |
| P5a vanishing inputs (consume P3b + 02KG) | ACTIVE | ~3–4 | ~200–400 | `cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`, INDEPENDENT of 02KG — only needs `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`; DISPATCHED iter-049); then `Hⁿ(f⁻¹V,G)` Ext-bridge + open-immersion/affine vanishing (these CONSUME `affine_serre_vanishing`). | Augmented-resolution lane is deep infra (stalk-local exactness of a sheaf complex + the augmented-complex object; mathlib-build, may hand off a decomposition). Ext-by-injective-resolution backing present (`InjectiveResolution.extEquivCohomologyClass`). |
| P5b comparison assembly | BLOCKED | ~2–3 | ~150–300 | P4 engine + P5a resolution + termwise `f_*`-acyclicity + EnoughInjectives connector. | Final Route-A assembly of the protected goal `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (line-679 sorry). Gated on P5a. |

## Completed

| Phase | Iters (done@ · used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 push–pull functor laws | 002 · 2 | ~120 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullMap_comp` closing push–pull functoriality | object-form align `simp [Functor.comp_obj]` before `reassoc_of%`; `rawPushPullMap`+`subst`+pentagon | `conjugateEquiv_comp` mate route INFEASIBLE (kernel `whnf` blow-up) |
| P2 `CechNerve`/`CechComplex` | 002 · 1 | ~60 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullFunctor`, `coverCechNerve(Aug)`, `CechNerve`, `CechComplex` axiom-clean | `Over.lift`+`.rightOp`+`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj`; terminal augmentation | none |
| P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (Leray, 015E) | 009 · 6 | ~965 | `AcyclicResolution.lean` | `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`, `rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic`, horseshoe `ofShortExact`, dimension shift — axiom-clean | decompose-then-build; two-step `cokernel.mapIso`; `Nat.rec` staircase off `stairGen` | `ShortComplex.mapCyclesIso` WRONG for left-exact functor; `← G.map_comp` silently fails by a mapped-complex term |
| P3 standard-cover Čech vanishing (section form, tilde) | 022 · ~14 | ~1200 | `CechAcyclic.lean` | `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` + `sectionCech_homology_exact` (IsZero homology p≥1) for `F=~M`; L1 tilde-bridge + L3 combinatorial core | accessors DEFEQ (`rfl`); abstract section maps (`set;clear_value`) before `IsLocalizedModule.ext`; target ABSOLUTE section complex | residual qcoh `F≅~ΓF` (01I8) deferred to 02KG consumer; old relative `affine` sorry superseded |
| P3b Čech↔derived bridge bricks | 025 · ~9 | ~1500 | `PresheafCech`,`FreePresheafComplex`,`CechBridge` | `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (free resolution of `O_𝒰`), `ses_cech_h1`, `injective_cech_acyclic` (pos-degree Čech vanishing for injectives) — axiom-clean | op-transport via `opFunctor`+`preadditiveYoneda`; `erw`+`.trans` for defeq-carrier mismatch; Route-B arrow-iso transfer | `maxHeartbeats 2000000` needed; LSP-staleness (trust only `lake env lean`) |
| Absolute-cohomology Form B + 01EO general basis criterion | 028 · 3 | ~600 | `AbsoluteCohomology`,`CechToCohomology` | `jShriekOU`=sheafify(free(yoneda U)); H⁰≅Γ (+naturality); 01EO chain L1–L4+top (`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`); `BasisCovSystem`/`HasVanishingHigherCech` cover-system encoding | Form B kills restriction-preserves-injectives (injective in 2nd Ext arg); cover-local presheaf form; `attribute [local instance] hasExtModules`; AB4* `Epi(Pi.map)` elementwise | `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` absent in Mathlib → carried as explicit hyp (P5a convention); 3 first-attempt iters, no churn |
| 01I8 `F≅~(ΓF)` via section-localization (Route B) | 048 · ~14 (040→048) | ~900 | `QcohTildeSections`,`QcohRestrictBasicOpen` | KEYSTONE `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule` (ρ_f localizes Γ); Route-B assembly `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` ⟹ **unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`**; tile chain B0–B4 + `qcoh_section_equalizer` + `isLocalizedModule_of_exact` | sheaf-axiom equalizer (NOT span-cover, which was circular); bundled `restrictScalars`-carrier for two-action tile descent; `change`/defeq for the `↑R`-Semiring diamond; basis-check via `IsCoverDense.iso_of_restrict_iso`+`specBasicOpen` | span-cover descent on global sections is CIRCULAR; tile lemma is NOT `restrict_obj`-rfl; `IsLocalizing`/`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing` absent; over-budget (~14 iters vs est ~2) — the single hardest sub-route |

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})`) is
(i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclic. The P4 abstract lemma
gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱf_* F` directly — ONE abstract lemma, NO spectral sequences. Acyclicity
input (ii) reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(affine, qcoh)=0`, supplied by the P3b bridge.

### Route SS — two spectral sequences (REJECTED)
Both absent from Mathlib (multi-thousand-LOC); rests on the same `injective_cech_acyclic` brick as A.
(Named "Route SS" to avoid collision with the 01I8-internal "Route B" section-localization route below.)

### The acyclicity bridge (torsor-free, load-bearing)
Route A's acyclicity reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(Spec A, qcoh)=0` (02KG). The honest
non-circular route is the minimal Čech↔derived bridge: (1) `injective_cech_acyclic`, (2)
`ses_cech_h1`, (3) the dimension-shift `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) consuming the
standard-cover Čech vanishing of P3 as its condition (3). All three bricks of (1)+(2) are done;
01EO is the next target. This breaks the circular regress: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.

### 01I8 affine `F≅~(ΓF)` — section-localization (Route B; the live route)
Selected over the global-presentation route (Route P), which reached the goal only through two
genuine-mathematics walls (`tilde_restrict_basicOpen` + `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` + qcoh-closed-under-
kernels). Goal: the keystone — for qcoh `F`, `IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (Γ(X,F)→Γ(D(f),F))` — then
`IsIso fromTildeΓ` (each `D(f)`-component IS `IsLocalizedModule.lift` of the keystone; via
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` essImage) ⟹ unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`. No `IsLocalizing` shortcut
(`IsLocalizing`/`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing` do NOT exist in Mathlib).

Two layers, both reuse the local tile structure:
- **Local tiles [DONE, B0–B4].** Per cover element `gⱼ`: a finite standard cover with `span{gⱼ}=⊤`
  (B1 `qcoh_finite_presentation_cover`), and a global `Presentation(modulesRestrictBasicOpen gⱼ F)` of
  each tile (B2 `presentationOverBasicOpen` → B3 engine `modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence` + object iso
  `overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` → B4 `presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen`). Non-circularity pitfall: the
  tile identification `F|_{D(gⱼ)}≅~Mⱼ` comes from tilde RIGHT-exactness (left adjoint preserves the
  presentation cokernel), NOT a left-exact `Γ(D(gⱼ),-)` of an abstract cokernel (which would need affine
  `H¹` and reopen the 02KG circularity). `QcohRestrictBasicOpen` is REUSED here (NOT dormant);
  `TildeExactness` stays dormant.
- **Keystone glue [ACTIVE] — sheaf-axiom equalizer (Stacks 01HV(4); DECIDED iter-041).** The original
  span-cover descent (`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover` on global sections) is CIRCULAR — its per-`gⱼ`
  hypothesis is the abstract `LocalizedModule(powers gⱼ)Γ(X,F)`, i.e. keystone-at-`gⱼ`, the same
  statement (confirmed `analogies/keystone-descent.md`). Correct route: localize F's degree-0/1 sheaf
  equalizer `0→Γ(X,F)→∏Γ(D(gⱼ),F)→∏Γ(D(gⱼgₖ),F)` at `f` (exactness of localization,
  `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`), match it term-by-term against the `D(f)`-cover equalizer via the
  per-tile localizations (DONE tile lemma on the tilde tiles — never on global `Γ(X,F)`), kernel
  comparison ⟹ keystone. Decomposed into `qcoh_section_equalizer`, `tile_section_localization`,
  `qcoh_section_kernel_comparison`. Non-circular because the only "sections localize" inputs sit on the
  tiles; the global step is F's free sheaf axiom + exact localization.

### Absolute cohomology realization — Ext of the corepresenting object (Form B)
`H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)` where `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`
is the corepresenting object of `F ↦ Γ(U,F) = F(U)` (`= j_!O_U` up to iso), built from the
already-shipped `freeYonedaHomEquiv` + `sheafificationAdjunction` (~50–80 LOC). The three facts
01EO consumes are then off-the-shelf with the SES staying in `X.Modules`: injective vanishing
`Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (injective `I` as the 2nd arg — no restriction taken), covariant LES
`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` at fixed 1st arg `jShriekOU U`, and `H⁰ ≅ Γ(U,F)` via the
corepresentability chain. The discarded alternatives: Form A `Ext(O_U, F|_U)` needs
restriction-preserves-injectives (≈ a 200–500 LOC `j_!` functor); `Functor.rightDerived` has
free injective vanishing but no packaged LES; `Sheaf.H` is absent. Reversal signal: Ext
universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), never `Sheaf.H`.

## Open strategic questions

- **EnoughInjectives connector (cone obligation).** The frozen target provides
  `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` but the 01EO/02KG cone carries the stronger
  `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]`. Connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` is TRUE,
  ~6 LOC (deg-0 mono into `I.cocomplex.X 0` via `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι`).
  Build + register at the P5b assembly lane — DISSOLVES the "EnoughInjectives absent in Mathlib" worry.
- P5a last-mile bridge `Hᵏ((f_*I^•)(V)) = Ext^k(jShriek(f⁻¹V), G)`: Ext-by-injective-resolution-of-2nd-arg
  backing CONFIRMED present (`InjectiveResolution.extEquivCohomologyClass`). Due when P5a consumers dispatch.
- 02KG `surj_of_vanishing` route: `ses_cech_h1` + `O_X`-epi local section surjectivity via
  `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective`, gated on the `toSheaf` epi-preservation BUILD (= `PreservesFiniteColimits
  (SheafOfModules.toSheaf)`, route confirmed, `analogies/tosheaf-epi.md`); refine the lift cover to affine
  opens (`standard_cover_cofinal`, done) to land a standard cover in `Cov`.
- Keystone glue: RESOLVED iter-041 (sheaf-axiom equalizer; see Routes `### 01I8`). Span-cover descent on
  global sections was circular; `analogies/keystone-descent.md` has the full non-circularity argument.
- Chapter cleanup (non-blocking): `lem:qcoh_localized_sections` is circular by the old span-cover
  mechanism but DORMANT (no DAG path to the main theorem); re-route or delete in a future writer pass.
- 01I8 section-localization route detail in Routes (`### 01I8 affine F≅~(ΓF)`). The `IsLocalizing`
  shortcut is OFF the table (analogist `bridge`: neither `IsLocalizing` nor `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing`
  exists). Assembly uses the explicit per-`D(f)`-component `IsLocalizedModule.lift` identity (Mathlib
  `Tilde.fromTildeΓ`) + `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` (essImage form) — confirm the iso-reflection at assembly.

## Mathlib gaps & new material

Gaps to fill (project-side, Mathlib lacks):
- Standard-cover Čech complex exactness `0→M→∏M_{f_i}→⋯` (P3) — done via L1+L3 around `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
- Presheaf-level Čech machinery for `O_X`-modules (P3b free/section complexes, `injective_cech_acyclic`,
  `ses_cech_h1`) — done. Dropped as off-critical-path: presheaf enough-injectives + δ-functor universality.
- Absolute module-valued `Hⁿ(U,F)` = `Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` via `Abelian.Ext` (Form B); corepresenting
  object `jShriekOU = sheafify(free(yoneda U))`. NOT a bespoke `j_!` functor (that is absent, 200–500 LOC).
- Relative/open-immersion affine vanishing `R^q(jₛ)_*=0`, `R^i(affine)_*=0` (P5).
- EnoughInjectives connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` (~6 LOC, instance) — see
  Open questions; makes the 01EO/02KG cone type-check against the frozen target's weaker hypothesis.
- `PreservesFiniteColimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf)` — the missing dual of Mathlib's `PreservesFiniteLimits`
  (toSheaf right-exactness), NOT a small instance: ~80–150 LOC via the sheafification square + left-adjoint
  reflector, never through `forget` (`analogies/tosheaf-epi.md`). `toSheaf` epi-preservation is then a
  corollary, unlocking `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective` for `surj_of_vanishing`.
- 01I8 instance `[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` via Route B — **DONE (iter-048, axiom-clean)**:
  local tiles B0–B4 + sheaf-axiom equalizer + keystone `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule` + assembly
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` ⟹ `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` UNCONDITIONAL for qcoh `F`. Closed via the
  basis-check `IsCoverDense.iso_of_restrict_iso`+`specBasicOpen`, each `D(f)`-component an
  `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv` of two localizations. (Route phase moved to `## Completed`.)

New project material:
- push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `p ↦ p_* p^* F` — hand-rolled.
- `CechNerve`/`CechComplex`; `AcyclicResolution.lean` (P4); P3b free-presheaf + section Čech complexes,
  `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`, `injective_cech_acyclic`, `ses_cech_h1`.
- `AbsoluteCohomology.lean`: `jShriekOU`, corepresentability iso, `H^p := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, -)`, H⁰=Γ,
  injective-vanishing + LES wrappers → 01EO `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` → 02KG `affine_serre_vanishing`.
- comparison assembly `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` via Route A.
