content:
# Strategy

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak existence
form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), with `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 02KG `affine_serre_vanishing` (cover-system + affine instantiation) | ACTIVE (ungated iter-049) | ~1 | ~40–120 | Tops `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` (02KG seed) + `affine_serre_vanishing` consume the now-unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (01I8 closed iter-048). Both DISPATCHED iter-049 (same lane, seed→top). | Cover-system chain COMPLETE; both blocks blueprint-gate-cleared (reviewer iter049). Seed = transport P3 tilde-vanishing along `F≅~M`; top = instantiate 01EO basis criterion. `[EnoughInjectives]` carried as explicit hyp (connector deferred to P5b). |
| P5a vanishing inputs (consume P3b + 02KG) | ACTIVE | ~3–4 | ~200–400 | `cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`, INDEPENDENT of 02KG — only needs `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`; DISPATCHED iter-049); then `Hⁿ(f⁻¹V,G)` Ext-bridge + open-immersion/affine vanishing (these CONSUME `affine_serre_vanishing`). | Augmented-resolution lane is deep infra (stalk-local exactness of a sheaf complex + the augmented-complex object; mathlib-build, may hand off a decomposition). Ext-by-injective-resolution backing present (`InjectiveResolution.extEquivCohomologyClass`). |
| P5b comparison assembly | BLOCKED | ~2–3 | ~150–300 | P4 engine + P5a resolution + termwise `f_*`-acyclicity + EnoughInjectives connector. | Final Route-A assembly of the protected goal `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (line-679 sorry). Gated on P5a. |

## Completed

| Phase | Iters (done@ · used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 push–pull functor laws | 002 · 2 | ~120 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullMap_comp` closing push–pull functoriality | object-form align `simp [Functor.comp_obj]` before `reassoc_of%`; `rawPushPullMap`+`subst`+pentagon | `conjugateEquiv_comp` mate route INFEASIBLE (kernel `whnf` blow-up) |
| P2 `CechNerve`/`CechComplex` | 002 · 1 | ~60 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullFunctor`, `coverCechNerve(Aug)`, `CechNerve`, `CechComplex` axiom-clean | `Over.lift`+`.rightOp`+`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj`; terminal augmentation | none |
| P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (Leray, 015E) | 009 · 6 | ~965 | `AcyclicResolution.lean` | `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`, `rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic`, horseshoe `ofShortExact`, dimension shift — axiom-clean | decompose-then-build; two-step `cokernel.mapIso`; `Nat.rec` staircase off `stairGen` | `ShortComplex.mapCyclesIso` WRONG for left-exact functor; `← G.map_comp` silently fails by a mapped-complex term |
| P3 standard-cover Čech vanishing (section form, tilde) | 022 · ~14 | ~1200 | `CechAcyclic.lean` | `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` + `sectionCech_homology_exact` (IsZero homology p≥1) for `F=~M`; L1 tilde-bridge + L3 combinatorial core | accessors DEFEQ (`rfl`); abstract section maps (`set;clear_value`) before `IsLocalizedModule.ext`; target ABSOLUTE section complex | residual qcoh `F≅~ΓF` (01I8) deferred to 02KG consumer; old relative `affine` sorry superseded |
| P3b Čech↔derived bridge bricks | 025 · ~9 | ~1500 | `PresheafCech`,`FreePresheafComplex`,`CechBridge` | `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (free resolution of `O_𝒰`), `ses_cech_h1`, `injective_cech_acyclic` (pos-degree Čech vanishing for injectives) — axiom-clean | op-transport via `opFunctor`+`preadditiveYoneda`; `erw`+`.trans` for defeq-carrier mismatch; Route-B arrow-iso transfer | `maxHeartbeats 2000000` needed; LSP-staleness (trust only `lake env lean`) |
| Absolute-cohomology Form B + 01EO general basis criterion | 028 · 3 | ~600 | `AbsoluteCohomology`,`CechToCohomology` | `jShriekOU`=sheafify(free(yoneda U)); H⁰≅Γ (+naturality); 01EO chain L1–L4+top (`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`); `BasisCovSystem`/`HasVanishingHigherCech` cover-system encoding | Form B kills restriction-preserves-injectives (injective in 2nd Ext arg); cover-local presheaf form; `attribute [local instance] hasExtModules`; AB4* `Epi(Pi.map)` elementwise | `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` absent in Mathlib → carried as explicit hyp (P5a convention); 3 first-attempt iters, no churn |
| 01I8 `F≅~(ΓF)` via section-localization (Route B) | 048 · ~14 (040→048) | ~900 | `QcohTildeSections`,`QcohRestrictBasicOpen` | KEYSTONE `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule` (ρ_f localizes Γ); Route-B assembly `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` ⟹ **unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`**; tile chain B0–B4 + `qcoh_section_equalizer` + `isLocalizedModule_of_exact` | sheaf-axiom equalizer (NOT span-cover, which was circular); bundled `restrictScalars`-carrier for two-action tile descent; `change`/defeq for the `↑R`-Semiring diamond; basis-check via `IsCoverDense.iso_of_restrict_iso`+`specBasicOpen` | span-cover descent on global sections is CIRCULAR; tile lemma is NOT `restrict_obj`-rfl; `IsLocalizing`/`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing` absent; over-budget (~14 iters vs est ~2) — the single hardest sub-route |

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})`) is
(i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclic. The P4 abstract lemma
gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱf_* F` directly — ONE abstract lemma, NO spectral sequences. Acyclicity
input (ii) reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(affine, qcoh)=0`, supplied by the P3b bridge.

### Route SS — two spectral sequences (REJECTED)
Both absent from Mathlib (multi-thousand-LOC); rests on the same `injective_cech_acyclic` brick as A.
(Named "Route SS" to avoid collision with the 01I8-internal "Route B" section-localization route below.)

### The acyclicity bridge (torsor-free, load-bearing)
Route A's acyclicity reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(Spec A, qcoh)=0` (02KG). The honest
non-circular route is the minimal Čech↔derived bridge: (1) `injective_cech_acyclic`, (2)
`ses_cech_h1`, (3) the dimension-shift `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) consuming the
standard-cover Čech vanishing of P3 as its condition (3). All three bricks of (1)+(2) are done;
01EO is the next target. This breaks the circular regress: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.

### 01I8 affine `F≅~(ΓF)` — section-localization (Route B; the live route)
Selected over the global-presentation route (Route P), which reached the goal only through two
genuine-mathematics walls (`tilde_restrict_basicOpen` + `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` + qcoh-closed-under-
kernels). Goal: the keystone — for qcoh `F`, `IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (Γ(X,F)→Γ(D(f),F))` — then
`IsIso fromTildeΓ` (each `D(f)`-component IS `IsLocalizedModule.lift` of the keystone; via
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` essImage) ⟹ unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`. No `IsLocalizing` shortcut
(`IsLocalizing`/`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing` do NOT exist in Mathlib).

Two layers, both reuse the local tile structure:
- **Local tiles [DONE, B0–B4].** Per cover element `gⱼ`: a finite standard cover with `span{gⱼ}=⊤`
  (B1 `qcoh_finite_presentation_cover`), and a global `Presentation(modulesRestrictBasicOpen gⱼ F)` of
  each tile (B2 `presentationOverBasicOpen` → B3 engine `modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence` + object iso
  `overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` → B4 `presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen`). Non-circularity pitfall: the
  tile identification `F|_{D(gⱼ)}≅~Mⱼ` comes from tilde RIGHT-exactness (left adjoint preserves the
  presentation cokernel), NOT a left-exact `Γ(D(gⱼ),-)` of an abstract cokernel (which would need affine
  `H¹` and reopen the 02KG circularity). `QcohRestrictBasicOpen` is REUSED here (NOT dormant);
  `TildeExactness` stays dormant.
- **Keystone glue [ACTIVE] — sheaf-axiom equalizer (Stacks 01HV(4); DECIDED iter-041).** The original
  span-cover descent (`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover` on global sections) is CIRCULAR — its per-`gⱼ`
  hypothesis is the abstract `LocalizedModule(powers gⱼ)Γ(X,F)`, i.e. keystone-at-`gⱼ`, the same
  statement (confirmed `analogies/keystone-descent.md`). Correct route: localize F's degree-0/1 sheaf
  equalizer `0→Γ(X,F)→∏Γ(D(gⱼ),F)→∏Γ(D(gⱼgₖ),F)` at `f` (exactness of localization,
  `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`), match it term-by-term against the `D(f)`-cover equalizer via the
  per-tile localizations (DONE tile lemma on the tilde tiles — never on global `Γ(X,F)`), kernel
  comparison ⟹ keystone. Decomposed into `qcoh_section_equalizer`, `tile_section_localization`,
  `qcoh_section_kernel_comparison`. Non-circular because the only "sections localize" inputs sit on the
  tiles; the global step is F's free sheaf axiom + exact localization.

### Absolute cohomology realization — Ext of the corepresenting object (Form B)
`H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)` where `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`
is the corepresenting object of `F ↦ Γ(U,F) = F(U)` (`= j_!O_U` up to iso), built from the
already-shipped `freeYonedaHomEquiv` + `sheafificationAdjunction` (~50–80 LOC). The three facts
01EO consumes are then off-the-shelf with the SES staying in `X.Modules`: injective vanishing
`Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (injective `I` as the 2nd arg — no restriction taken), covariant LES
`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` at fixed 1st arg `jShriekOU U`, and `H⁰ ≅ Γ(U,F)` via the
corepresentability chain. The discarded alternatives: Form A `Ext(O_U, F|_U)` needs
restriction-preserves-injectives (≈ a 200–500 LOC `j_!` functor); `Functor.rightDerived` has
free injective vanishing but no packaged LES; `Sheaf.H` is absent. Reversal signal: Ext
universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), never `Sheaf.H`.

## Open strategic questions

- **EnoughInjectives connector (cone obligation).** The frozen target provides
  `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` but the 01EO/02KG cone carries the stronger
  `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]`. Connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` is TRUE,
  ~6 LOC (deg-0 mono into `I.cocomplex.X 0` via `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι`).
  Build + register at the P5b assembly lane — DISSOLVES the "EnoughInjectives absent in Mathlib" worry.
- P5a last-mile bridge `Hᵏ((f_*I^•)(V)) = Ext^k(jShriek(f⁻¹V), G)`: Ext-by-injective-resolution-of-2nd-arg
  backing CONFIRMED present (`InjectiveResolution.extEquivCohomologyClass`). Due when P5a consumers dispatch.
- 02KG `surj_of_vanishing` route: `ses_cech_h1` + `O_X`-epi local section surjectivity via
  `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective`, gated on the `toSheaf` epi-preservation BUILD (= `PreservesFiniteColimits
  (SheafOfModules.toSheaf)`, route confirmed, `analogies/tosheaf-epi.md`); refine the lift cover to affine
  opens (`standard_cover_cofinal`, done) to land a standard cover in `Cov`.
- Keystone glue: RESOLVED iter-041 (sheaf-axiom equalizer; see Routes `### 01I8`). Span-cover descent on
  global sections was circular; `analogies/keystone-descent.md` has the full non-circularity argument.
- Chapter cleanup (non-blocking): `lem:qcoh_localized_sections` is circular by the old span-cover
  mechanism but DORMANT (no DAG path to the main theorem); re-route or delete in a future writer pass.
- 01I8 section-localization route detail in Routes (`### 01I8 affine F≅~(ΓF)`). The `IsLocalizing`
  shortcut is OFF the table (analogist `bridge`: neither `IsLocalizing` nor `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing`
  exists). Assembly uses the explicit per-`D(f)`-component `IsLocalizedModule.lift` identity (Mathlib
  `Tilde.fromTildeΓ`) + `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` (essImage form) — confirm the iso-reflection at assembly.

## Mathlib gaps & new material

Gaps to fill (project-side, Mathlib lacks):
- Standard-cover Čech complex exactness `0→M→∏M_{f_i}→⋯` (P3) — done via L1+L3 around `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
- Presheaf-level Čech machinery for `O_X`-modules (P3b free/section complexes, `injective_cech_acyclic`,
  `ses_cech_h1`) — done. Dropped as off-critical-path: presheaf enough-injectives + δ-functor universality.
- Absolute module-valued `Hⁿ(U,F)` = `Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` via `Abelian.Ext` (Form B); corepresenting
  object `jShriekOU = sheafify(free(yoneda U))`. NOT a bespoke `j_!` functor (that is absent, 200–500 LOC).
- Relative/open-immersion affine vanishing `R^q(jₛ)_*=0`, `R^i(affine)_*=0` (P5).
- EnoughInjectives connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` (~6 LOC, instance) — see
  Open questions; makes the 01EO/02KG cone type-check against the frozen target's weaker hypothesis.
- `PreservesFiniteColimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf)` — the missing dual of Mathlib's `PreservesFiniteLimits`
  (toSheaf right-exactness), NOT a small instance: ~80–150 LOC via the sheafification square + left-adjoint
  reflector, never through `forget` (`analogies/tosheaf-epi.md`). `toSheaf` epi-preservation is then a
  corollary, unlocking `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective` for `surj_of_vanishing`.
- 01I8 instance `[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` via Route B — **DONE (iter-048, axiom-clean)**:
  local tiles B0–B4 + sheaf-axiom equalizer + keystone `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule` + assembly
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` ⟹ `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` UNCONDITIONAL for qcoh `F`. Closed via the
  basis-check `IsCoverDense.iso_of_restrict_iso`+`specBasicOpen`, each `D(f)`-component an
  `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv` of two localizations. (Route phase moved to `## Completed`.)

New project material:
- push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `p ↦ p_* p^* F` — hand-rolled.
- `CechNerve`/`CechComplex`; `AcyclicResolution.lean` (P4); P3b free-presheaf + section Čech complexes,
  `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`, `injective_cech_acyclic`, `ses_cech_h1`.
- `AbsoluteCohomology.lean`: `jShriekOU`, corepresentability iso, `H^p := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, -)`, H⁰=Γ,
  injective-vanishing + LES wrappers → 01EO `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` → 02KG `affine_serre_vanishing`.
- comparison assembly `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` via Route A.

# Project Progress

## Current Stage

prover

## Stages
- [x] init
- [x] autoformalize
- [ ] prover
- [ ] polish

## End-state overview

**Zero inline `sorry` in the dependency cone of the protected declaration
`AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage`, kernel-only axioms, 0 project
axioms.** Sole goal: close the Čech computation of higher direct images
(`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`) and its cone. Full framing in STRATEGY.md.

## Iter-049 context — 01I8 CLOSED (iter-048); FAN-OUT begins (02KG + P5a augmented resolution)

- **iter-048 SOLVED the LAST 01I8 step:** `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` (the Route-B assembly)
  LANDED axiom-clean (`#print axioms` = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}), registered as an
  `instance`. **`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F` is now UNCONDITIONAL for quasi-coherent `F`.** This is the
  terminal milestone of the Route B section-localization program (iters 040→048). +1 private component helper
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_app_basicOpen` (each `D(r)`-component = `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv` of the two
  localizations `tilde.toOpen` and `ρ_r`-keystone). lean-auditor `iter048` + lean-vs-blueprint `iter048-qts`:
  0 must-fix. File `QcohTildeSections.lean` 0-sorry.
- **The downstream Route-A cone is now OPEN.** The single-critical-path constraint (a property of the pre-01I8
  keystone chain) is dissolved. THIS ITER fans out two independent ready lanes (blueprint HARD GATE cleared for
  both, blueprint-reviewer `iter049`: 0 must-fix):
  1. **02KG** — `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` (seed) → `affine_serre_vanishing` (top), same file.
  2. **P5a** — `cechAugmented_exact` (augmented Čech complex is a resolution; INDEPENDENT of 02KG —
     only needs the now-unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`).
- **Project inline-sorry = 2**, both frozen/superseded: `CechHigherDirectImage.lean:679` (protected P5b
  assembly), `CechAcyclic.lean:110` (dead `affine`). Build GREEN (iter-048).

## The keystone chain (01I8 / Route B) — DONE (iters 040→048), see STRATEGY `## Completed`
- Tile leaves B0–B4 + `qcoh_section_equalizer` + base-ring descent + `tile_section_localization` (iter-041→046);
  KEYSTONE `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule` + `qcoh_section_kernel_comparison` + abstract
  `isLocalizedModule_of_exact` (iter-047); Route-B assembly `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` (iter-048).
  All axiom-clean. `QcohTildeSections.lean` + `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean` are DONE (0 sorries) — no prover.
- **Recurring recipe (KB [[rR-semiring-diamond-change-workaround]]):** over basicOpen sections never `rw`/`simp`
  on `∘ₗ`/`LinearMap.pi`/`hom_comp` (CommRing→Semiring diamond) — use `change`(defeq) + presheaf-abstracted
  helpers via `refine (…).trans ?_`, `@`-threaded instances. Sheaf-iso-on-basis: `.hom` not `.val`,
  `IsCoverDense.iso_of_restrict_iso` + `specBasicOpen` space-pinning, `[instance]` args via `haveI`.

## THIS ITER — the two fan-out lanes

### Lane 1 (02KG, CRITICAL PATH) — `AffineSerreVanishing.lean`
- **`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh`** (`lem:affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh`) — 02KG seed (Stacks condition (3)).
  For qcoh `F` on `Spec R`: vanishing higher Čech cohomology for the affine cover system, i.e.
  `Ȟᵖ(𝒰,F)=0` for every standard cover and `p>0`. **Proof:** by `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (now unconditional)
  `F ≅ ~M`, `M=Γ(X,F)`; the P3 standard-cover tilde vanishing `cech_acyclic_affine`
  (`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`) applies to `~M`; transport the section-Čech-complex iso along `F≅~M`.
- **`affine_serre_vanishing`** (`lem:affine_serre_vanishing`) — 02KG top (Serre vanishing): for affine `U`
  and qcoh `F`, `Hᵖ(U,F)=0` for `p>0`. **Proof:** instantiate the 01EO basis criterion
  `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (`cech_to_cohomology_on_basis`) at `affineCoverSystem` (3 fields already
  discharged: `affine_faces_mem`/`affine_surj_of_vanishing`/`affine_injective_acyclic`); condition (3) is the
  seed above; take `V=U`. Carries `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as explicit hyp (connector deferred to P5b).
- Both decls **do not yet exist** in the file (the header hands them off to "the assembly iteration"). Same
  file ⟹ ONE lane, seed first then top (the top `\uses` the seed).

### Lane 2 (P5a, INDEPENDENT) — `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`
- **`cechAugmented_exact`** (`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`) — the augmented Čech complex
  `0→F→C⁰→C¹→⋯` (`Cᵖ = (j_{i₀…i_p})_*(F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})`, the degree-`p` object of the Čech nerve
  `coverCechNerveOverAug`/`CechNerve`) is **exact** — i.e. the Čech nerve is a resolution of `F` in QCoh(X).
  This is geometric input (i) of the Route-A comparison (the line-679 P5b assembly consumes it).
  **Proof (stalk-at-prime; blueprint sketch rewritten iter-049 to this path):** exactness of a complex of
  sheaves is checked stalkwise; on an affine `U=Spec A`, `F|_U≅~M` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`), so the
  localization at a prime `𝔭` is the localized extended complex `0→M_𝔭→∏(M_{f_i})_𝔭→⋯`; since `{f_i}` cover,
  some `f_i∉𝔭` is a unit in `A_𝔭`, giving the standard contracting homotopy = the P3 standard-cover vanishing
  `cech_acyclic_affine`. Holds at every prime ⟹ exact on `X`.

## Current Objectives

TWO independent `mathlib-build` lanes (the 01I8 close opened the downstream cone). Both are **build of NEW
decls** that do not yet exist — no-sorry mathlib-build, NOT sorry-fills. Blueprint HARD GATE cleared this iter
(blueprint-reviewer `iter049`: complete + correct, 0 must-fix; the Lane-2 sketch was aligned to stalk-at-prime
by blueprint-writer `cechaug` + purified by blueprint-clean `cechaug`).

1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean`** — build the new declarations for `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` then `affine_serre_vanishing` (which **does not yet exist** in this file; a no-sorry mathlib-build of new decls, NOT sorry-fills). [prover-mode: mathlib-build]
   - **SOURCE OF TRUTH:** blueprint `lem:affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` (~line 6045) and `lem:affine_serre_vanishing` (~line 3206) in `chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` (the consolidated chapter `% archon:covers` this file). Read both blocks' statements + proof sketches first.
   - **DISPATCH ORDER (same lane):** build `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` FIRST (the top `\uses` it), then `affine_serre_vanishing` consuming it.
   - **SEED `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh`:** the cover-system fields are ALREADY built in this file (`affineCoverSystem`, `affine_faces_mem`, `affine_surj_of_vanishing`, `affine_injective_acyclic`, `standard_cover_cofinal`). The only new input is the now-unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F` (instance `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` makes `[IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]` resolve automatically for qcoh `F` — confirm via `inferInstance`). 903	  projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_i\) and post-composes with the presheaf
904	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\). Reading the
905	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate through the product equivalence (the coordinate-projection
906	  restatement \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\)) commutes the projection
907	  past the categorical sum: applying the sum of \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-morphisms to an element
908	  distributes over the sum (the finite-sum-application identity
909	  \(\operatorname{ab\_hom\_finsetSum\_apply}\)), and each summand becomes
910	  \((-1)^i\) times the face restriction of the \((\sigma \circ d_i)\)-coordinate. The
911	  per-coordinate projection of the cosimplicial coface is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\)
912	  computation \(\operatorname{Pi.lift\_π}\), which identifies the projected coface with
913	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) precomposed with the projection to
914	  \(\sigma \circ d_i\). Assembling the signs yields the stated alternating sum.
915	\end{proof}

919	[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
920	  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}

925	  Take \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), the standard affine cover \(\mathcal{U}\)
926	  associated with a spanning family \(s : \iota \to R\), and \(\mathcal{F} =
927	  \widetilde{M}\) the quasi-coherent sheaf associated with an \(R\)-module \(M\)
928	  (for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\), see the note following the statement). In
929	  the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
930	  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
931	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), read through the product
932	  equivalence, is exactly the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of the
933	  module complex \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}.
934	  Concretely, under the per-coordinate identifications each section group
935	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\) and each face
936	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) is the away-localisation
937	  coface \(\operatorname{dCoface}\), so the alternating sum of face restrictions of
938	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} becomes the alternating sum of localisation
939	  cofaces, i.e.\ \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
940	\end{lemma}
941	
942	% NOTE: For the tilde case \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) the per-coordinate
943	% comparison isomorphism and the naturality square follow directly from
944	% \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\). For a general quasi-coherent
945	% \(\mathcal{F}\), reduce to the tilde case via \(\mathcal{F} \cong
946	% \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag 01I8), the affine equivalence
947	% \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\),
948	% which is the open gap recorded in Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
949	\begin{proof}
950	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
951	  The proof layers in two steps.
952	
953	  \emph{(i) Abstract unfold.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the
954	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate of the degree-\(p\) differential, read through the product
955	  equivalence, is the alternating sum
956	  \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) of presheaf face
957	  restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates. This fixes the shape of the
958	  differential without any sheaf identification of the restrictions.
959	
960	  \emph{(ii) Tilde \(\mathcal{F}\)-bridge.} For \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) each
961	  face restriction is identified with the localisation coface
962	  \(\operatorname{SectionCechModule.dCoface}\) by transporting along a per-coordinate
963	  isomorphism. The section group
964	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localised module
965	  \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}
966	  s_\sigma)\, M\) both localise \(M\) at the submonoid of powers of
967	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\): the former by
968	  \(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\) together with the multi-index
969	  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) (which rewrites
970	  \(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\) as the single basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\)), the latter
971	  by construction. Hence there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
972	  \[
973	    \varphi_\sigma :
974	      \operatorname{ToType}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\bigr)
975	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
976	      M_{s_\sigma},
977	  \]
978	  the comparison of the two \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) structures, unique by
979	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) and supplied by
980	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\). The naturality square
981	  \[
982	    \varphi_\sigma \;\circ\; \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
983	      \;=\;
984	      \operatorname{dCoface} \;\circ\; \varphi_{\sigma \circ d_i}
985	  \]
986	  is exactly \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) of
987	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the presheaf restriction of
988	  \(\widetilde{M}\) between the two section groups is the away-localisation comparison
989	  of the two localisation units. This face-naturality is recorded in two forms: at the
990	  bare localisation level as \(\operatorname{phiL\_naturality}\) and at the sheaf level
991	  (with \(\varphi_\sigma = \operatorname{phi}\)) as \(\operatorname{phi\_naturality}\).
992	  Substituting (ii) into (i) and summing with signs
993	  identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
994	
995	  The isomorphism \(\varphi_\sigma\) is obtained by matching the two descriptions of
996	  the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(s_\sigma)\): the cosimplicial functor
997	  provides one description and the localisation property of
998	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} provides the other. Their agreement,
999	  together with the naturality square, is the content of this bridge.
1000	\end{proof}

1002	\begin{lemma}
1003	[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
1004	  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
1005	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact,
1006	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact,
1007	    AlgebraicGeometry.ab_hom_finsetSum_apply,
1008	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionToModuleAddEquiv,
1009	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply,
1010	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply}
1011	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
1012	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match,
1013	    lem:section_cech_module_exact}
1014	  For \(1 \leq p\) the homology of the section {\v C}ech complex
1015	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
1016	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} vanishes,
1017	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1018	  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\). The argument splits into a homological
1019	  reduction and a ladder transport:
1020	  \begin{itemize}
1021	    \item \emph{Reduction.} By standard homological algebra, vanishing of the
1022	      homology at degree \(q+1\) is equivalent to exactness of the short complex
1023	      around that node, and hence to function exactness of the two consecutive
1024	      coface differentials \(d^q\), \(d^{q+1}\) as maps of abelian groups.
1025	    \item \emph{Ladder transport.} The degreewise additive isomorphism
1026	      (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}) between the section complex and
1027	      the localised-module complex of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}
1028	      transports the \(R\)-module exactness across an additive ladder, yielding
1029	      function exactness for the group-level coface differentials.
1030	  \end{itemize}
1031	\end{lemma}
1032	
1033	\begin{proof}
1034	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
1035	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
1036	  Fix \(p = q + 1 \geq 1\). By standard homological algebra it suffices to show that
1037	  the two consecutive coface differentials \(d^q\), \(d^{q+1}\) of the section
1038	  complex are exact as maps of abelian groups (exactness at a node iff vanishing
1039	  homology, via the short complex around it).
1040	
1041	  The function exactness is established by an additive ladder transport. The
1042	  vertical maps of the ladder are the degreewise additive isomorphisms
1043	  \(e_p\): the product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
1044	  composed with the coordinatewise comparison \(\varphi_\sigma\) that identifies
1045	  each section group with the localised module \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The two
1046	  commuting squares of the ladder follow from
1047	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} (the differential unfolds as an
1048	  alternating sum of face restrictions) and Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
1049	  (each face restriction equals the localisation coface), giving
1050	  \(e_{p+1} \circ d^p = d_M^{\,p} \circ e_p\). The horizontal exactness is
1051	  the \(R\)-module exactness of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact};
1052	  transporting it across the additive ladder yields function exactness for the
1053	  coface differentials of the section complex.
1054	\end{proof}

1085	  R}\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(s : \iota \to R\) (\(\iota\) finite) a spanning
1086	  family of the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) =
1087	  \top\) (Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}). The \emph{un-localised section
1088	  {\v C}ech module complex} \(D^\bullet\) is the cochain complex of \(R\)-modules
1089	  whose degree-\(p\) term is the product over multi-indices
1090	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of the away localisations
1091	  \[
1092	    D^p \;=\; \prod_{\sigma} M_{s_\sigma},
1093	    \qquad s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
1094	    \qquad M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}
1095	      \bigl(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\bigr)\, M,
1096	  \]
1097	  with differential \(d^p : D^p \to D^{p+1}\) the alternating sum
1098	  \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j\, \delta^j\) of the coface comparison maps \(\delta^j\), each of
1099	  which is the canonical away-localisation comparison
1100	  \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\) sending a fraction to its image under
1101	  the further localisation at the omitted factor. The single-coefficient comparison
1102	  map and its localisation property are recorded by the \(\mathtt{dCoface}\) and
1103	  \(\mathtt{dCoeff}\) data and the differential by \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) (with action
1104	  \(\mathtt{dDiff\_apply}\)); the auxiliary \(\mathtt{spanIdx}\)/\(\mathtt{spanIdx\_spec}\)
1105	  select a witnessing index of the spanning relation.
1106	
1107	  The complex \(D^\bullet\) is tied to the section {\v C}ech complex of
1108	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and to its localisations by three
1109	  comparison families. First, the maps \(\mathtt{dToCech}\) identify each term
1110	  \(D^p\) with the degree-\(p\) section group
1111	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) of
1112	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} as a localised module
1113	  (\(\mathtt{dToCech\_isLocalizedModule}\)), intertwining the differentials
1114	  (\(\mathtt{cechCoface\_dToCech}\), \(\mathtt{dToCech\_comm}\)); the section-side
1115	  cofaces are \(\mathtt{cechCofaceLin}\) with action \(\mathtt{cechCoface\_apply}\).
1116	  Second, localising \(D^\bullet\) at a spanning element \(s_r\) reproduces the
1117	  \emph{localised} {\v C}ech complex: the localised differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
1118	  (action \(\mathtt{locDiff\_apply}\)) agrees, via
1119	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\), with the dependent-coefficient combinatorial
1120	  differential, and its exactness is recorded as \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\). The
1121	  localisation comparison maps \(\mathtt{fLoc}\) (action \(\mathtt{fLoc\_apply}\))
1122	  exhibit each localised term as a localised module
1123	  (\(\mathtt{fLoc\_isLocalizedModule}\)) intertwining \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) with
1124	  \(\mathtt{locDiff}\) (\(\mathtt{locDiff\_fLoc}\), \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\)).
1125	  The key algebraic fact underlying all three families is the localisation
1126	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\): localising the away module \(M_{s_\sigma}\)
1127	  further at \(s_r\) gives \(M_{s_r s_\sigma}\). This is the keystone
1128	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\) (the composite of two away
1129	  localisations is again the away localisation at the product), with the cancellation
1130	  bookkeeping \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.Inverts.smul\_pow\_cancel}\). Thus localising
1131	  \(D^\bullet\) at \(s_r\) (an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.Away}\)) reproduces
1132	  the localised Čech complex of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, and
1133	  exactness of \(D^\bullet\) can be checked one localisation at a time.

1136	\begin{lemma}
1137	
1138	\leanok
1139	[Positive-degree exactness of the section {\v C}ech module complex]
1140	  \label{lem:section_cech_module_exact}
1141	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact}
1142	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1143	  With the notation of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, the
1144	  un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\) is exact in positive
1145	  degrees: for every \(p\),
1146	  \[
1147	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(d^{p+1} : D^{p+1} \to D^{p+2},\;
1148	      d^{p+2} : D^{p+2} \to D^{p+3}\bigr).
1149	  \]
1150	  This is step~(a) of the affine vanishing: the node-by-node exactness of the
1151	  un-localised module complex, from which the homology vanishing of the section
1152	  {\v C}ech complex (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, step~(c)) is read off.
1153	\end{lemma}
1154	
1155	\begin{proof}
1156	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1157	  \leanok
1158	  Exactness of \(D^\bullet\) at each positive-degree node is a local-to-global
1159	  statement over the spanning family. By the local-to-global exactness criterion
1160	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\) (with the
1161	  spanning hypothesis \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\)), it
1162	  suffices to verify \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p+1}, d^{p+2})\) after
1163	  localising at each spanning element \(s_r\). In the localisation at \(s_r\) the
1164	  comparison family \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\) identifies the localised
1165	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) with the localised Čech differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
1166	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} (using the localisation
1167	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\) of
1168	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\)). Exactness of that
1169	  localised differential is \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\), which is in turn the
1170	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial exactness transported along
1171	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\); the contracting homotopy at the fixed index
1172	  \(r\) (where \(s_r\) is invertible) furnishes \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\),
1173	  giving the localised exactness one localisation at a time. Reassembling over the
1174	  spanning family yields the un-localised exactness.
1175	\end{proof}

1280	  \paragraph{Reduction to the localised complex.}

1282	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap D(s_{\sigma(p)})\)

1324	  and \(\mathtt{hcomm}\) hold for the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\). The node is
1325	  then closed by \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of the dependent combinatorial port,
1326	  which packages the identity \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\) into the
1327	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) statement that

1344	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1345	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1346	
1347	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1348	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1349	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1350	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1351	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1352	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1353	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1354	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1355	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1356	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1357	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1358	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1359	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1360	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1361	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1362	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1363	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1364	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1365	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1366	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1367	\(\delta\)-functor identification.

7131	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
7132	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
7133	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
7134	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
7135	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
7136	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
7137	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
7138	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
7139	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
7140	  \[
7141	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
7142	      \;\cong\;
7143	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
7144	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
7145	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},

7147	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
7148	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
7149	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
7150	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
7151	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
7152	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
7153	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
7154	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
7155	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
7156	
7157	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
7158	  of the preimage,
7159	  \[
7160	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)

7173	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
7174	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
7175	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in

Spanning hypothesis met ⟹ `D_{R_f}^•` is exact in positive degrees. **Correct** — `dDiff_exact` is polymorphic in the base ring (no whole-space spanning required; only over the ring it is invoked on).
    - *Step 2 (degreewise AddEquiv ladder):* For each multi-index tuple σ: `D(g_σ) ⊆ D(f)` ⟹ `g_σ ∈ √(f)` ⟹ `f` is invertible in `M_{g_σ}`. Transitivity `lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule` (`AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule`, already in `CechAcyclic.lean` line ~608) gives `M_{g_σ} ≅ (M_f)_{g_σ}`. These per-tuple AddEquivs form a ladder between the R-side and R_f-side complexes, commuting with the alternating-sum differentials (naturality of the comparison maps in the localisation base). Exactness transfers across the ladder. **Correct** — the invertibility witness `g_σ ∈ √(f)` is standard topology (`V(g_σ) ⊆ V(f)` iff `g_σ ∈ √(f)`); the naturality square is exactly `lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule`'s naturality claim.
    - *Step 3 (exactness → IsZero homology):* Wrap function exactness of the R-side complex into `IsZero(Ȟ^p) = 0` for `p > 0` via the homology reduction in the spirit of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`. **Correct** — the direction "positive-degree function exactness ⟹ IsZero(homology)" is a general algebraic fact; the proof sketch correctly identifies this as the closing wrap.

Proof: pull the open-cover equality back along `Spec(R_f) ≅ D(f) ↪ Spec R` (the comap of `algebraMap R R_f`); comap commutes with D(-) and ⊔; pullback of `D(f)` is all of `Spec R_f` (since `f` is a unit in `R_f`); so `⊔_i D(g_i/1) = Spec R_f`; apply `lem:iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff_span`. **Correct** and self-contained.

Already a project declaration (`CechAcyclic.lean` line ~608 `AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule`). The blueprint block's derivation note ("when `g_σ ∈ √(f)`, localising `M` away from `g_σ` and localising `M_f` away from `g_σ` present the same module") is correct.
  - **`lem:cech_augmented_resolution` (CechHigherDirectImage.lean PROVER TARGET) — complete + correct.** Statement: the augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` is exact (a resolution of F in QCoh(X)). `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}`. Proof sketch:
